settings.subheading: A Guide to Crafting Your Own Ceremonial Edit
settings.title: The Art Of The Ceremonial Stack
settings.description:
The beauty of ceremonial jewellery is that it doesn’t have to be a singular choice, but an evolving one; rings that mark promises, milestones, and memories.

Stacked over time, your ceremonial collection becomes a deeply personal reflection of your journey. Here, we break down the ways you can approach building your own.

settings.content_intro:
DO: BUY WHAT YOU LOVE

Natalie: Simply, a piece of vintage jewellery should be chosen because it resonates with you, and because you fall in love with it. Not only are they imbued with the histories and stories of their past owners, but vintage jewellery also provides an opportunity to embrace sustainable purchasing habits – offering once-cherished designs a second (or third) lease on life.

I have always had a love for vintage jewellery, or vintage anything really. I used to spend hours in op shops collecting all sorts of odd finds – furniture, clothes, hats, even once a vintage suitcase which I used to transport and display my first ever collection! I love the mystery and enigmatic nature of vintage pieces, the opportunity for speculation on their history and journey. I also have a deep reverence for the incredible craftsmanship many of these piece’s hold. So much tradition and heritage exists in every tiny detail, which is where my love for vintage jewellery originates from.

I encourage you to find your spark. Is it a particular stone colour or cut, the setting or design style or maybe even the piece’s provenance and era that speaks to you? Is it because it reminds you of a place, someone special or loved ones from generations past? I always like to remind clients that jewellery doesn’t have to be engraved or marked with a special stone to become solely yours – it can be anything about the piece that sparks a feeling or takes you back to a moment in time.

There’s a sense of romance and allure inherit in vintage pieces, and finding the perfect design should feel akin to falling in love: a lightning bolt of recognition; the sense that you two were destined to be connected.

settings.description:
Start with Meaning

Before starting, or adding to, your stack, take a second to reflect on what’s already there. What made you choose your current jewellery favourites? What memories or milestones do they hold? Every new addition or ring should feel like a continuation of your story – whether in exact harmony or through striking contrast.


Experiment with Form and Texture

Mixing metals, stones, and silhouettes can evoke an unexpected depth to your stack. Softly contoured bands might accentuate angular engagement rings, while bold gold pieces can add warmth and dimension. The addition of colour – whether a birthstone or a hue that simply speaks to you – can alter the mood of the mix in a way that feels entirely your own.
settings.description_mobile:
Start with Meaning


Before starting, or adding to, your stack, take a second to reflect on what’s already there.What made you choose your current jewellery favourites? What memories or milestones do they hold? Every new addition or ring should feel like a continuation of your story – whether in exact harmony or through striking contrast.

Experiment with Form and Texture


Mixing metals, stones, and silhouettes can evoke an unexpected depth to your stack. Softly contoured bands might accentuate angular engagement rings, while bold gold pieces can add warmth and dimension. The addition of colour – whether a birthstone or a hue that simply speaks to you – can alter the mood of the mix in a way that feels entirely your own.

settings.title: Eternal bonds, symbolic of vows taken. Ceremonial stacks are gestures of forever, exchanged in harmony with the one you love.

settings.description:
Mark the Moments

At NMJ we have long believed that love isn’t defined by a single day – and your ceremonial stack doesn’t have to be either. From anniversaries to new beginnings or quiet personal victories – each one can be honoured with a meaningful addition such as a delicate engraving, new stone, or a band that symbolises strength or change. It’s sometimes the smallest details that turn your stack into something ever-evolving.

Follow Your Instincts

Just as there’s no template or one-size-fits-all blueprint for a relationship, there’s no formula for layering. A ceremonial stack is personal, fluid, and deeply individual, so trust your eyes, your hands, and your heart. Whether a vintage-inspired band or a a modern sculptural piece, let yourself be drawn to what feels right. The magic is in the mix.

settings.description:
Guiding Thoughts For Your Beginning

We invite you to reflect on the symbols, milestones, and emotions that define your unique love story.

Whether you're dreaming up a future addition to your stack or envisioning a ring that captures this very moment, take a pause to explore the meaning behind the pieces you wear—and the ones yet to come.

  • What symbols, words, or stones hold the most meaning to you and your love story?
  • How has your definition of commitment, love, or partnership evolved over time?
  • If you were to design a ring that represents this exact moment in your life, what would it look like?
  • What is one milestone – big or small – you would love to commemorate with a future addition to your stack?

settings.description:
At NMJ, we believe that jewellery is an investment – something to love, wear and treasure forever. And while we are passionate about creating modern heirlooms for our clients, we also have a soft spot for vintage jewellery.

Immortalising nostalgia, vintage designs remain an integral source of inspiration for our founder Natalie Fitch, who believes that by breathing new life into time-worn pieces, we are also able to foster a more conscious, circular approach to purchasing. “I love the mystery and enigmatic nature of vintage pieces; the opportunity for speculation on their history and journey,” she says. However, for the uninitiated, the prospect of shopping pre-loved treasures can be daunting. What are some key features to look out for? How do you know you’ve found the one?

On the NMJ Journal, we ask our in-house expert – our founder, Natalie Fitch – for her guide to purchasing vintage, how to care for your finds and what sparked her love for jewellery with a past.


DO: BUY WHAT YOU LOVE

Natalie: Simply, a piece of vintage jewellery should be chosen because it resonates with you, and because you fall in love with it. Not only are they imbued with the histories and stories of their past owners, but vintage jewellery also provides an opportunity to embrace sustainable purchasing habits – offering once-cherished designs a second (or third) lease on life.

I have always had a love for vintage jewellery, or vintage anything really. I used to spend hours in op shops collecting all sorts of odd finds – furniture, clothes, hats, even once a vintage suitcase which I used to transport and display my first ever collection! I love the mystery and enigmatic nature of vintage pieces, the opportunity for speculation on their history and journey. I also have a deep reverence for the incredible craftsmanship many of these piece’s hold. So much tradition and heritage exists in every tiny detail, which is where my love for vintage jewellery originates from.

I encourage you to find your spark. Is it a particular stone colour or cut, the setting or design style or maybe even the piece’s provenance and era that speaks to you? Is it because it reminds you of a place, someone special or loved ones from generations past? I always like to remind clients that jewellery doesn’t have to be engraved or marked with a special stone to become solely yours – it can be anything about the piece that sparks a feeling or takes you back to a moment in time.

There’s a sense of romance and allure inherit in vintage pieces, and finding the perfect design should feel akin to falling in love: a lightning bolt of recognition; the sense that you two were destined to be connected.

settings.feature_text: It rained and it poured on our wedding day! But it was perfect in every way.

settings.title: NMJ Wedding
settings.description: The Wedding Collective is a thoughtful curation of ceremonial treasures and wedding jewellery. Created in celebration of love, it is our tailored offering of precious tokens, each laced with sentiment and intended for marking precious moments.
